title: Backend: POST /api/admin/deploy that shells out to bin/deploy.sh
userStory: As Steve, I want a server endpoint that runs the deploy script so that the PWA can trigger an upgrade without me SSHing.
acceptanceCriteria: POST starts a background `bin/deploy.sh` execution and returns 202 immediately with { run_id, started_at }
Auth: same admin-scoped cookie/bearer as /admin (NOT the regular chat session); reject otherwise with 401
Concurrency lock: if a run is already in-flight, return 409 with the in-flight run_id (no overlapping deploys)
Process output streamed line-by-line to a Postgres table brain.deploy_runs (id, started_at, finished_at, status in [running|success|failed], exit_code, log TEXT, triggered_by)
Deploy script path resolved from env BRAINBOT_DIR (default /opt/brainbot); script invoked with timeout (e.g. 5 min) — kill + mark failed on timeout

Verify: curl -X POST -H 'admin auth' /api/admin/deploy returns 202 with run_id; second call within the run window returns 409; brain.deploy_runs row transitions running -> success after the script completes

references.name: Coolify deploy trigger
references.what: Reference for: returning 202 with a run id, persisting log lines as they stream, lock semantics for in-flight runs.

references.name: OpenClaw self-deploy endpoint
references.what: Reference for: shape of the deploy_runs table, how the run lock is acquired/released across container restarts.
